items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').
Another classic faux ami. Spanish 'constipado' refers to nasal congestion (a cold). The medical 'constipated' is 'estreñido'. Confusion can lead to awkward medical conversations.

items.q: What is the gender of 'el día' (day)?
items.why: Students rely on '-a = feminine'. Common exceptions: el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el clima (many Greek-origin -ma words). These appear constantly so must be memorised.
Correct answer: Volunteering. “Salary” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Madres de Plaza de Mayo are from?
items.why: It's Argentina. “Chile” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Madres de Plaza de Mayo first march?
items.why: Exact answer: 1977. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
